channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Cointelegraph - Security, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading, Blockthreat, CryptoPotato, CryptoSlate, Krebs On Security, The Hacker News, Coindesk, GroupIB Blog, Data Breach Today, CNN.com - Crypto News, DataBreaches.net

item.title: New BioShocking attack manipulates AI browser into data theft
item.description: A new prompt injection attack dubbed "BioShocking" could trick AI-powered browsers into treating real-world risky actions as part of a fictional scenario, causing them to ignore any safety guardrails....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Fake Bug Report Hijacks AI Coding Agents at Scale
item.description: "Agentjacking" is the latest demonstration of how easily attackers can exploit an AI agent's inability to differentiate between content and instructions....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Microsoft accelerates quantum-safe roadmap as risks grow
item.description: Microsoft announced today that it is accelerating its quantum-safe security roadmap, saying advances in quantum computing are bringing the need to replace today's encryption standards sooner than prev...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Critics Say BIP-110 Could Break Self-Custody and Risk User Funds
item.description: Existing Bitcoin software supporting BIP-110 may reportedly create addresses that become invalid after the proposed upgrade....
item.dc:creator: CryptoPotato

item.title: Malicious PyPI packages give hackers control of Telegram bot servers
item.description: A campaign active since last November has been targeting Python developers building Telegram bots with trojanized Pyrogram forks that allow attackers to read arbitrary files on compromised servers. [....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.description: Attackers don't need any special authentication to reach a target endpoint — they just need to know where it is....

item.title: Microsoft Warns Poisoned MCP Tool Descriptions Can Make AI Agents Leak Data
item.description: New Microsoft research shows how attackers can hijack AI agents that act on a user's behalf, using nothing more than a poisoned tool description to make the agent quietly hand over company data to an ...

item.title: RustDuck Botnet Rebuilds in Rust to Hijack Routers and Servers for DDoS
item.description: A new two-stage malware family called RustDuck is hijacking home routers, IP cameras, Android boxes, and poorly secured servers, then stitching them into a network built to knock websites and online s...

item.title: Langflow RCE Exploited to Deploy Monero Miner on Exposed AI App Endpoints
item.description:
Threat actors are continuing to exploit a critical Langflow vulnerability as part of fresh attacks designed to deliver a Monero cryptocurrency miner.
The activity has been found to weaponize CVE-2026...

item.description: A malicious extension in the Chrome Web Store is masquerading as the Perplexity AI answer engine, intercepting search traffic and collecting browsing information. [...]...

item.title: GuardFall Exposes Open-Source AI Coding Agents to Decades-Old Shell Injection Risks
item.description:
The safety check that is supposed to stop an AI coding agent from running a dangerous command can be walked straight past using a shell trick that has been public for decades.

item.title: 282 iOS AI Apps Leak API Keys and Open AI Proxy Access in Network Traffic Study
item.description:
Researchers tested 444 AI chatbot apps for iPhone and found that 282 of them, nearly two-thirds, exposed paid AI access through their network traffic.
